description: Trigger-accuracy and behavioural evals. Includes test 8 cross-skill chain — the novel work of Session 4 (first exercise of validate-upstream mode in code).

evals.prompt: We need to decide whether to hire an SDR or invest in marketing automation for Q3 lead-gen. I'm leaning toward the SDR but want to stress-test.
expectations: Skill runs anti-anchoring guard with hypothesis_provenance: operator branch
Output includes single verdict (PROCEED / DEFER / REFRAME)
If HIGH confidence: output includes specific-observable falsifier

evals.prompt: I just ran /diagnose-bottleneck and it identified legal contract negotiation as the constraint. Now I'm deciding: hire another lawyer, automate templates, or restructure to bypass legal. Can you adjudicate?
expectations: Skill recognises chain handoff (hypothesis_provenance should be prior_skill)
anti_anchoring.verdict is validate_upstream_stable OR validate_upstream_unstable, NOT AGREED/DISAGREED/INCONCLUSIVE

evals.prompt: Where is the bottleneck in our pipeline right now? We have throughput numbers per stage.
Why has our MRR plateaued after 9 months of 15% growth?
We have three options: A, B, C. Which one is best?
expectations: Skill detects AP3 (menu without preferred option)
Skill redirects to brainstorming or /council, not this skill

evals.prompt: Should the brand logo be navy or charcoal?
expectations: Skill refuses jurisdiction (pure aesthetic / values; no discriminating observable)

behavioural_tests_reference: See ## Tests section in SKILL.md for the 8 acceptance test cases per spec §9 (tests 1-7 mandatory per shared library; test 8 novel cross-skill chain test added by Phase 3.5 rule patch — first exercise of validate-upstream mode in code).
